review: Sonnet found no blocking issues. The documentation field-list overclaim was corrected during orchestration acceptance. Remaining non-blocking observations: the implementation report's 'full crew DTO rendering' framing is broader than the actual partial read-only summary (consistent with the F2b character-detail precedent), and the /crew/:id route regex accepts any hyphenated alphanumeric id rather than only UUIDs (falls through to a 404/decode-error path, matching character-detail's identical pattern).
notes: Crew detail page reuses existing Crew DTO schema and backend endpoint (GET /api/crews/{id} from A2). Page rendering follows character-detail.ts pattern: Effect-based error handling, aria-live, loading/error/success states. Router extends main.ts to match /crew/:id and dispatch to crew detail page. Roster page updated with links to crew detail pages. Acceptance agent independently reran `npm test -- --run` (27/27) and `npm run build`, then spun up a fresh isolated backend-ada instance (temp data/games dirs seeded from the repo's data/games fixtures on port 9758), created a crew via POST /api/crews, confirmed GET /api/crews/{id} returns a decodable Crew DTO, /crew/{id} serves the SPA shell, and an unknown id returns 404; the exact server PID was stopped afterward and the temp dir removed. No forbidden paths (contract/, conformance/, blades-in-the-sheets/) touched. Crew detail page renders crew name, type, basics (lair, hunting grounds, reputation), status (tier, hold, heat, wanted, rep), fund (coin, stash), and notes sections.
